title: Why this chronicle matters now
summary: Gengchen Mai's research trajectory demonstrates how geographic information science evolved into GeoAI. His work on spatially explicit AI, geo-foundation models, and geographic bias addresses critical challenges at the intersection of AI and spatial data science.

signals.label: SEAI Lab at UT Austin
signals.text: Founded and directs the Spatially Explicit Artificial Intelligence Lab, focusing on GeoAI, geo-foundation models, and geographic knowledge graphs.

signals.label: NSF Grant for Geographic Bias Research
signals.text: Awarded $300,000 NSF grant in 2025 to develop frameworks for measuring and mitigating geographic bias in AI models and foundation models.

signals.label: Google Research Collaboration
signals.text: Serves as Visiting Faculty Researcher at Google Research, bridging academic research with industry applications in GeoAI.

signals.label: Recognition in GeoAI Community
signals.text: Received AAG Nystrom Award (2023) and William L. Garrison Award (2022) for contributions to geographic representation learning and GeoAI.

nextMoves.label: Trustworthy GeoAI
nextMoves.text: Developing statistics-informed frameworks to quantify and mitigate geographic bias in AI models, addressing an often-overlooked challenge in ethical AI development.

nextMoves.label: Geo-Foundation Models
nextMoves.text: Advancing research on foundation models for geospatial data, enabling more generalizable and robust AI systems for spatial analysis.

nextMoves.label: Geographic Knowledge Graphs
nextMoves.text: Building structured knowledge representations that capture spatial relationships and geographic context for AI applications.
